title: SEP Enrichment Envelope
description: The core schema for the Stream Exchange Protocol (SEP). Every content item in a SEP stream carries an enrichment envelope containing source metadata, semantic enrichment scores, emotional tone analysis, session-fit indicators, and provider attestation. This is the foundational data structure that enables consumer-side algorithmic sovereignty.

sep_version.description: The version of the Stream Exchange Protocol this envelope conforms to.

item_id.description: A globally unique identifier for this content item within the SEP ecosystem.

content_type.description: The type of content this item represents.

emotional_tone_value.description: A classification of emotional tone detected in the content.

unit_interval.description: A normalized score between 0 and 1 inclusive.

category.description: A semantic category assigned to the content with a confidence score indicating the strength of the classification.

id.description: The category identifier from the enrichment taxonomy (e.g., 'fitness', 'humor', 'craft', 'skill_building').

confidence.description: Confidence that this category applies to the content.

source.description: Origin information for the content item. Points back to the original platform and resource. SEP is meta-first: it references content rather than hosting it.

platform.description: The platform where the content originates (e.g., 'youtube', 'spotify', 'substack', 'rss').

origin_url.description: The canonical URL of the content on its origin platform.

origin_id.description: The platform-specific unique identifier for the content (e.g., a YouTube video ID).

duration_seconds.description: The duration of the content in seconds. Use 0 for content without a fixed duration (e.g., articles).

meta.description: Human-readable metadata about the content item, sourced from the origin platform and normalized for SEP consumption.

title.description: The title of the content item as published on the origin platform.

creator.description: The name of the content creator or channel.

published.description: The publication timestamp of the content on its origin platform, in ISO 8601 format.

original_tags.description: Tags or keywords from the origin platform, preserved as-is for provenance.

language.description: The primary language of the content as a BCP 47 language tag.

thumbnail_url.description: URL of the content thumbnail image on the origin platform.

emotional_tone.description: Emotional tone analysis of the content. Includes a primary and optional secondary tone classification, plus boolean flags for content that may be harmful or manipulative.

primary.description: The dominant emotional tone of the content.

secondary.description: A secondary emotional tone, if present. Null if the content has a single dominant tone.

rage_bait.description: Whether the content is designed to provoke anger for engagement.

humiliation.description: Whether the content derives entertainment value from humiliating individuals.

shock_content.description: Whether the content relies on shock value to drive engagement.

inflammatory.description: Whether the content is designed to inflame divisions or provoke heated reactions.

sexually_explicit.description: Whether the content contains sexually explicit material.

violence.description: Whether the content contains graphic violence or glorifies violent behavior.

session_fit.description: Indicates where this content item fits within a composed session arc. Session arcs give structure to content consumption — opening with lighter content, building through skill and focus, peaking with intensity, and closing with calm.

good_opener.description: Whether this item works well at the start of a session (e.g., light humor, warm-up energy).

good_builder.description: Whether this item works well in the middle of a session (e.g., skill-building, focused learning).

good_peak.description: Whether this item works well as a session peak (e.g., high motivation, intensity spike).

good_closer.description: Whether this item works well to close a session (e.g., calm craft, reflective content, pump-up music).

enrichment.description: The semantic enrichment layer produced by the LLM-powered enrichment pipeline. Contains all machine-generated scores, classifications, and session-fit indicators that enable consumer-side algorithmic sovereignty.

schema_version.description: The version of the enrichment schema used to produce these scores. Enrichment schemas are versioned independently from the SEP protocol version.

enriched_at.description: Timestamp when the enrichment was performed, in ISO 8601 format.

categories.description: Semantic categories assigned to this content, ordered by confidence descending.

energy_level.description: The overall energy level of the content, from 0 (very calm, meditative) to 1 (very high energy, intense).

cognitive_load.description: The cognitive effort required to consume this content, from 0 (passive, easy) to 1 (dense, demanding).

motivation_score.description: How motivating or action-inspiring the content is, from 0 (neutral) to 1 (highly motivating).

humor_score.description: The humor content level, from 0 (no humor) to 1 (primarily comedic).

skill_transfer_score.description: How much actionable skill or knowledge the content transfers, from 0 (pure entertainment) to 1 (highly instructional).

production_quality.description: Assessment of the production quality (audio, video, editing, presentation), from 0 (low quality) to 1 (professional production).

provider.description: Attestation from the stream provider that served this enrichment envelope. Includes the provider identity and whether the content passed the provider's guardrail checks.

id.description: The unique identifier of the stream provider that produced this envelope.

guardrail_pass.description: Whether this content item passed the provider's guardrail checks. A value of false indicates the item was flagged but included for transparency.

guardrail_version.description: The version of the guardrail ruleset applied during evaluation.
